title: Legends of the Northwest
description: Legends of the Northwest — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Legends of the Northwest" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Algonkin
memes.description: 'The Algonkin (Algonquian) language family spoken by the Ojibway, Chippewa, and related peoples of the Great Lakes region'

memes.label: "Algonkin Tribes"
memes.description: 'The Algonkin-speaking tribes of the Great Lakes, including the Ojibway, Ottawa, and Potawatomi'

memes.label: Anpétu-Sâpa
memes.description: 'The Dark Sun spirit — Anpétu-Sâpa — that haunts the Falls of St. Anthony in Dakota belief, appearing in the Legend of the Falls as a tragic female figure'

memes.label: Anpétuwee
memes.description: 'The Dakota sun deity — the more powerful of the twin sky brothers, father of life, issuing every morning from the lodge of Han-nan-na to journey across the sky'

memes.label: Assiniboin
memes.description: 'The Assiniboins or Hó-hé (Stone-roasters), a tribe speaking the Dakota tongue who originally split from the Dakota nation over a legendary feud. (016-040)'

memes.label: Big Thunder
memes.description: 'Wakínyan Tânka (Big Thunder), Dakota chief of the Kapóza band, father of Little Crow, also a mythological thunder-being in Dakota cosmology'

memes.label: Bison
memes.description: 'The American bison, central to Dakota subsistence and culture'

memes.label: Black Robes
memes.description: 'The Jesuit missionary priests called "Black Robes" by the Dakotas for the color of their vestments, active at La Pointe on Lake Superior from the 1660s (013-048).'

memes.label: Calumet
memes.description: 'The ceremonial pipe used by Dakota and other tribes — documented by Marquette and cited by Gordon as a principal element of Dakota custom, used at feasts and diplomatic encounters'

memes.label: Canoe
memes.description: 'The birch-bark canoe, essential to Dakota and Ojibway transportation'

memes.label: "Ceremonial Feast"
memes.description: 'The ceremonial feast tradition of the Dakota people, marking major communal events'

memes.label: Chaské
memes.description: 'A brave Dakota hunter, the devoted suitor and eventual husband of Wiwásté in "The Feast of the Virgins" legend — the first-born son known by this name in Dakota tradition.'

memes.label: Clan
memes.description: "The traditional kinship-based social unit of Dakota and Ojibway peoples, appearing in Gordon's legends as the organizational structure of warriors and chiefs"

memes.label: Claude Allouez
memes.description: 'Claude Allouez (Glaude Allouëz), French Jesuit missionary who permanently established the Mission of the Holy Ghost at La Pointe in 1665, the first enduring European mission in present-day Wisconsin'

memes.label: Cree
memes.description: 'The Cree (Knisteneaux) people — a major Algonquian-speaking Indigenous nation referred to as "Mastincapi" (Rabbits) by the Dakotas, appearing throughout Gordon's legends as allies of the Ojibways and traditional foes of the Dakotas'

memes.label: Dakota
memes.description: 'A confederation of Native American bands speaking a common tongue, known to French traders as "Sioux"'

memes.label: "Dakota Band"
memes.description: 'The individual bands that made up the Dakota Confederation, each with distinct territory and leadership'

memes.label: "Dakota Confederation"
memes.description: 'The confederation of Dakota bands united by a common language and origin — Dakota signifies alliance or confederation (P01-010)'

memes.label: "Dakota Council"
memes.description: 'The council of Dakota leaders that made decisions of war, peace, and alliance for the confederation'

memes.label: "Dakota Maiden"
memes.description: 'The Dakota maidens — including the fifty maidens of the Feast of the Virgins — who appear throughout the legends'

memes.label: Dakota Mythology
memes.description: 'The mythological system of the Dakota people as documented in Gordon''s Legends of the Northwest — encompassing the creation by Unktéhee, the sky-thunder conflict, and the spirit taxonomy recorded in the ethnographic footnotes'

memes.label: "Dakota-Ojibway War"
memes.description: "The inter-tribal conflict between the Dakota and Ojibway peoples that forms the dramatic backdrop of Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Dakota Prairie"
memes.description: "The vast grassland that was the ancestral home of the Dakota people, the setting of Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Dakota Warrior"
memes.description: "The unnamed Dakota warriors whose courage and traditions are central to Gordon's legends"

memes.label: Dance
memes.description: "Ceremonial dance in Dakota culture — a sacred communal practice central to the Feast of the Virgins, war preparation, and spiritual expression in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Daniel DuLuth"
memes.description: "Daniel Greysolon, Sieur du Lhut (c. 1639–1710) — French explorer and soldier, portrayed as 'DuLuth' in Gordon's Legends of the Northwest, a suitor of Winona in the poetic narrative."

memes.label: Death Song
memes.description: 'The ceremonial death song sung by Dakota warriors facing death in battle, a recurring motif in Gordon legends'

memes.label: "Distributed Proofreading Team"
memes.description: "The Project Gutenberg volunteer team that digitized and preserved Gordon's Legends of the Northwest (P01-001)"

memes.label: "DuLuth's Council"
memes.description: "The diplomatic council held by French explorer DuLuth with the Dakota braves, a historical event preserved in Gordon's Winona"

memes.label: "Edward D. Neill"
memes.description: 'Historian of Minnesota and the Dakota people, frequently cited in Gordon footnotes'

memes.label: "Explorers' Expedition"
memes.description: 'The French and British exploring expeditions that first documented the upper Mississippi region'

memes.label: "Falls of St. Anthony"
memes.description: "The great waterfall on the Mississippi River — Ha-Ha to the Dakotas — subject of The Legend of the Falls and a sacred site in Dakota cosmology"

memes.label: "Feast of the Virgins"
memes.description: "The major ceremonial gathering of the Dakota people — a multi-day festival of feasting, dance, and courtship central to Gordon's first legend cycle"
aliases: Wiwâstè sings at the Virgins Feast

memes.label: "Fort Snelling"
memes.description: "The U.S. military fort at the confluence of the Mississippi and Minnesota Rivers — the region the Dakota called Mendota (Mdó-tè)"

memes.label: Francis Parkman
memes.description: 'Francis Parkman, American historian whose book "Discovery of the Great West" is cited by Gordon as a source on Dakota mythology and the Thunder-Bird legend'

memes.label: "French Trading Posts"
memes.description: 'The network of French fur trading posts established by voyageurs along the Mississippi and its tributaries'

memes.label: Fur Trade
memes.description: 'Redirect: the fur trade in the upper Mississippi region, conducted by French voyageurs and trading companies with the Dakota people.'

memes.label: "Fur Trade Company"
memes.description: 'The fur trading companies that employed voyageurs and established commerce with the Dakota people'

memes.label: George Catlin
memes.description: 'George Catlin, American painter and author specializing in Native American portraits and customs; cited by Gordon as a source on the Dakota Sun-Dance ceremony'

memes.label: "Gordon's Publisher"
memes.description: "The publishing enterprise that brought Gordon's Legends of the Northwest to print in 1878"

memes.label: "Great Forest"
memes.description: 'The northern forests that were home to the Ojibway people, contrasting with the Dakota prairie'

memes.label: "The Great Hunt"
memes.description: 'The ceremonial buffalo hunt of the Dakota people, a central event in their subsistence and spiritual life'

memes.label: Ha-Ha
memes.description: 'The Dakota onomatopoeic name for the Falls of St. Anthony — "lightly laughing" water, rendered in Gordon''s poem as the refrain "Ha-ha, ha-ha, ha-ha, ha!"'

memes.label: Hanford Lennox Gordon
memes.description: Author of Legends of the Northwest (1878), poet and student of Dakota

memes.label: Hârpstinà
memes.description: 'Dakota maiden, the impetuous rival of Wiwâstè in "The Feast of the Virgins," who kindles a fatal revenge and dies consumed by guilt. (001-105)'

memes.label: Henry Longfellow
memes.description: 'Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, celebrated American poet whose "Evangeline" and "Hiawatha" were key literary influences on H. L. Gordon'

memes.label: Henry Schoolcraft
memes.description: 'Henry Rowe Schoolcraft, American ethnologist and geologist whose studies of Native American cultures and legends were source material for Gordon and Longfellow'

memes.label: "Henry H. Sibley"
memes.description: "First governor of Minnesota and figure in the Sioux War referenced in Gordon's footnotes"

memes.label: Heyóka
memes.description: 'The sacred clown and contrary spirit-being in Dakota mythology, universally feared and reverenced, honored through the severe Heyóka Wacípee dance ritual'

memes.label: "Historical Society"
memes.description: 'The Minnesota Historical Society and similar organizations that preserved records of Dakota history'

memes.label: Hóhé
memes.description: 'The Dakota name for the Assiniboin people, a Siouan-speaking group who split from the Dakotas and allied with the Ojibways — known as "Stone-roasters" for their distinctive cooking method'

memes.label: Holy Ghost Mission
memes.description: 'The Jesuit mission at La Pointe on Waug-a-bá-mé island in Cha-quam-egon Bay, founded about 1660 (018-099).'

memes.label: I-tó-ka-ga Wi-câs-ta
memes.description: 'The Dakota spirit or god of the South, the inveterate enemy of Wa-zí-ya (the North Wind), whose eternal war explains the cycle of the seasons. (015-207)'

memes.label: Isantee Band
memes.description: 'A principal band of the Dakota people, whose main village Kathága stood at the Falls of St. Anthony two hundred years ago (018-122).'

memes.label: Jacques Marquette
memes.description: 'Father Jacques Marquette, French Jesuit missionary and explorer who took over the La Pointe mission in 1669 and wrote early descriptions of the Dakota people'

memes.label: John Calhoun
memes.description: 'John C. Calhoun, U.S. Senator and Secretary of War for whom Lake Calhoun (now Bde Maka Ska) in Minneapolis was named'

memes.label: John Tanner
memes.description: 'John Tanner (c. 1780–1846), American frontiersman captured as a child and raised among the Ojibwe and Ottawa, whose captivity narrative is cited by Gordon as an ethnographic source on Algonkin customs and language'

memes.label: "Jonathan Carver"
memes.description: '18th-century explorer whose accounts of the Dakota informed later writers'

memes.label: Joseph Nicollet
memes.description: 'Joseph Nicollet, French explorer and cartographer who mapped the Upper Mississippi region; Nicollet Island in Minneapolis bears his name'

memes.label: Josiah Snelling
memes.description: 'Colonel Josiah Snelling, U.S. Army officer for whom Fort Snelling was named; author of "Tales of the Northwest" cited by Gordon'

memes.label: "Juliet Sutherland"
memes.description: 'A producer of the Project Gutenberg digital edition of Legends of the Northwest'

memes.label: Kapóza
memes.description: 'The principal village of the Light-foot (Kapóza) band of Dakotas, located near the Falls of St. Anthony — home of chief Little Crow and central to the Winona legend cycle.'

memes.label: Kapóza
memes.description: 'The principal village of the Light-foot (Kapóza) band of Dakotas, originally located on the east bank of the Mississippi below the mouth of the Minnesota River — a central hub of Dakota life and trade'

memes.label: Ke-úk-sa
memes.description: 'The principal village of the hereditary Wapasa chiefs, located at the site of present-day Winona, Minnesota; its name means "the village of law-breakers." (016-015)'

memes.label: "Lake Pepin"
memes.description: "A natural widening of the Mississippi River, significant in the geography of Gordon's Dakota legends"

memes.label: "Lake Superior"
memes.description: "The Great Lake — Gitchee Gumee in Ojibway, Medé Tânka in Dakota — home of the Ojibways and setting of The Sea-Gull legend in Gordon's collection"

memes.label: Legend
memes.description: 'The Dakota and Ojibway oral legend tradition that H. L. Gordon collected and rendered into English verse'

memes.label: Legend of the Falls
memes.description: "Gordon's narrative poem recounting the Dakota legend of the Falls of St. Anthony — the story of Anpétu-Sâpa (Dark Sun), her lover, and the catastrophic rupture that created the cataract"

memes.label: Little Crow
memes.description: 'Ta-ó-ya-te-dú-ta (Little Crow), Dakota chief of the Kapóza band who led warriors in the Minnesota outbreak of 1862–63'

memes.label: Lodge
memes.description: "Traditional Dakota and Ojibway dwelling and ceremonial structure, appearing throughout Gordon's legends as both a physical home and a spiritual space"

memes.label: "Louis Hennepin"
memes.description: 'Father Louis Hennepin — Franciscan missionary and early European explorer of the upper Mississippi'

memes.label: Mary Eastman
memes.description: "Mary Henderson Eastman (Mrs. Eastman), author of \"Dacotah; or, Life and Legends of the Sioux,\" a key ethnographic source for Gordon's footnotes"

memes.label: Medicine-Man
memes.description: 'The Dakota spiritual healer and magician — Wicasta Wakan — who casts out unclean spirits, handles fire without injury, and belongs to the secret order of the Sons of Unktéhee'

memes.label: Medicine Pouch
memes.description: 'The magical pouch of the spirits given by Unktéhee to the Dakota, a sacred object in Dakota spiritual tradition'

memes.label: "Mendota"
memes.description: "The Dakota name for the confluence of the Mississippi and Minnesota Rivers — Mdó-tè, meaning 'outlet of lake or river into another' (009-024) — site of Fort Snelling"

memes.label: "Michabo"
memes.description: 'The Great Spirit of the Algonkin peoples, smoking his pipe in Ojibway legend'

memes.label: "Mille Lacs"
memes.description: "Mille Lacs — Mdé Waksân (Spirit Lake) in Dakota — a sacred lake and major Dakota settlement, discovered by DuLuth in 1679"

memes.label: "Minnesota"
memes.description: "The land of the Dakota people — 'fair forests and fertile fields' (P01-171) — the geographic heartland of Gordon's Legends of the Northwest"

memes.label: "Minnetonka"
memes.description: "The lake that gives its name to the final poem in Gordon's collection, a place of beauty in Dakota country"

memes.label: "Missionary Society"
memes.description: 'The missionary organizations that sent Riggs and others to work among the Dakotas'

memes.label: "Mississippi Flood"
memes.description: 'The seasonal flooding of the Mississippi River, a natural event with spiritual significance'

memes.label: "Mississippi River"
memes.description: "The great river at the heart of Gordon's Prelude — Michesee Seebee in Ojibway (010-116) — the central waterway of Dakota country"

memes.label: Moccasin
memes.description: "Traditional soft-soled footwear of the Dakota and Ojibway peoples, appearing in ceremonial and domestic contexts in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: Net-nó-kwa
memes.description: 'Net-nó-kwa (The Otter Woman), an Ottawa female chief (Oge-mâ-kwa) of the Red River region who adopted John Tanner as her son, cited in the Notes and Appendix of "Legends of the Northwest" as an example of female leadership among Algonkin tribes'

memes.label: Nicollet Island
memes.description: 'A Mississippi River island near the Falls of St. Anthony, known to the Dakota as "Wi-ta Waste" (Beautiful Island) and featured in the legend of Winona.'

memes.label: Ojibway
memes.description: 'An Algonkin-speaking Native American people, also called Chippewa, rivals of the Dakotas in Gordon legends'

memes.label: "Ojibway Chief"
memes.description: 'The unnamed Ojibway leaders who appear as antagonists in the Dakota legend cycles'

memes.label: "Ojibway Nation"
memes.description: "The Algonkin-speaking Ojibway (Chippewa) nation of the Great Lakes region — rivals of the Dakotas and central figures in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Ojibway Raid"
memes.description: 'A surprise attack by Ojibway warriors on a Dakota village — an event at the boundary of warfare and legend'

memes.label: Panther
memes.description: 'A Dakota warrior and hunter, central figure in "The Legend of the Falls" — husband first to the Sea-Gull and later to the Red Fox.'

memes.label: "Pepin Lake"
memes.description: 'Lake Pepin, a wide stretch of the Mississippi between Minnesota and Wisconsin, featured in Dakota legends'

memes.label: "Pictured Rocks"
memes.description: 'The Pictured Rocks of Lake Superior, the setting of the Ojibwa legend of The Sea-Gull'

memes.label: Prairie
memes.description: 'The prairie landscape, home of the Dakotas and setting of Gordon legends'

memes.label: "Project Gutenberg"
memes.description: 'The digital library project that produced the electronic edition of Legends of the Northwest'

memes.label: "Prophet's Vision"
memes.description: 'A visionary experience by a Dakota holy man, an event at the boundary of spiritual and historical'

memes.label: Raven
memes.description: 'Son of Panther and the Sea-Gull, called Kâk-kâh-gè — a central figure in the latter part of "The Legend of the Falls."'

memes.label: Red Cloud
memes.description: 'A tall, proud Dakota warrior — central figure in "The Feast of the Virgins" legend, known for his fierce pride and vengeful jealousy.'

memes.label: Red Fox
memes.description: 'A crafty Dakota woman, the second wife of Panther and rival to the Sea-Gull — the antagonist figure in "The Legend of the Falls."'

memes.label: Red River
memes.description: 'The Red River of the North, called the "Bloody River" in Gordon''s legends, site of numerous intertribal battles between the Dakota and Chippewa.'

memes.label: René Menard
memes.description: 'Father René Menard, the first Jesuit missionary in the Lake Superior region, who appears in the Winona legend as the aged "Black Robe"'

memes.label: "Riggs Mission"
memes.description: 'The missionary enterprise of Reverend S. R. Riggs among the Dakotas, which produced key ethnographic records'

memes.label: "Rocky Mountains"
memes.description: 'The distant mountains marking the western edge of the territory known to the Dakotas'

memes.label: Samuel W. Pond
memes.description: 'Rev. Samuel W. Pond, one of the first Protestant missionaries to the Dakota people at Shakopee, and a linguistic authority cited by Gordon'

memes.label: "Saskatchewan"
memes.description: "The far northern river where 'the degenerate remnant' of the Dakotas now sleeps (P01-049)"

memes.label: Scalping
memes.description: 'The practice of taking an enemy scalp as a war trophy in Dakota and Ojibway warfare, depicted in Gordon legends'

memes.label: Sea-Gull
memes.description: A central figure in "The Legend of the Falls" — first wife of Panther,

memes.description: "Front Matter & Preface — Legends of the Northwest by H. L. Gordon"

memes.description: "The Feast of the Virgins — Part I — Legends of the Northwest by H. L. Gordon"

memes.label: "Winona — Part I"
memes.description: "Winona — Part I — Legends of the Northwest by H. L. Gordon"

memes.label: "Winona — Part II"
memes.description: "Winona — Part II — Legends of the Northwest by H. L. Gordon"

memes.label: "Winona — Part III"
memes.description: "Winona — Part III — Legends of the Northwest by H. L. Gordon"

memes.label: Según
memes.description: 'The fair youth who personifies Summer in "The Legend of the Falls" — the foe of Winter whose arrival in the North-land brings warmth, renewal, and seasonal transformation.'

memes.label: Shingebís
memes.description: 'A water-fowl (hell-diver) that remains on the frontiers all winter. In the "Legend of the Falls," Shingebís alone defies the Winter-maker, sitting and laughing at Ka-be-bón-ík-ka while all other birds flee south (014-074–076; 018-187 fn).'

memes.label: "Sioux Delegation"
memes.description: 'The Dakota delegations that negotiated with European and American authorities'

memes.label: Song
memes.description: "Ceremonial and narrative songs in Dakota and Ojibway oral traditions — the primary medium of cultural memory preserved in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: "The immortal spiritual essence of a person in Dakota and Ojibway belief, appearing in Gordon's legends through themes of death, immortality, and the afterlife"

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: "Supernatural beings and the spirit realm in Dakota and Ojibway belief systems — central to the cosmology of Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Spirit Ceremony"
memes.description: 'Ceremonies invoking the spirit world, conducted by Dakota medicine men'

memes.label: "Spirit Island"
memes.description: 'An island in the Mississippi with spiritual significance in Dakota tradition'

memes.label: "St. Anthony Falls"
memes.description: "Redirect: alternate name for the Falls of St. Anthony — see [[falls-of-st-anthony]] for the full article."

memes.label: "St. Louis River"
memes.description: 'The river bearing an Ojibway name (010-119), a tributary in the Great Lakes watershed'

memes.label: Stephen R. Riggs
memes.description: Reverend Stephen Return Riggs — missionary to the Dakotas for forty years,

memes.label: Superstition
memes.description: 'The traditional beliefs, superstitions, and spiritual practices of the Dakota people as studied by H. L. Gordon'

memes.label: "Susan Skinner"
memes.description: 'A producer of the Project Gutenberg digital edition of Legends of the Northwest'

memes.label: Sweat Lodge
memes.description: 'A Dakota purification ceremony using a steam-tight domed structure. Heated stones are placed inside and water poured over them to create steam. Called E-né-pee in Dakota, it was used as preparation before sacred feasts (018-050–051).'

memes.label: Ta-té-psin
memes.description: 'An aged Dakota brave, father of Winona — a figure of paternal authority and tragic loss in the "Winona" legend cycle.'

memes.label: Tamdóka
memes.description: 'A Dakota warrior, son of Wazí-kuté, suitor of Winona — portrayed as fleet-footed, haughty, and crafty in Gordon''s "Winona" legend.'

memes.label: Thunder-Bird
memes.description: 'The immense supernatural bird of Dakota mythology whose wing-flapping produces thunder, also a hereditary chief title — Wakínyan Tanka (Big Thunder)'

memes.label: Tomahawk
memes.description: 'The traditional war-club or axe of the Dakota and Ojibway warriors, a cultural symbol appearing in Gordon legends'

memes.label: Tradition
memes.description: 'The customs and traditions of the Dakota people, a primary subject of Gordon ethnographic study'

memes.label: "Treaty Signing"
memes.description: 'The formal agreements between Dakota bands and European powers, events marking the end of Dakota sovereignty'

memes.label: "Tribal Council"
memes.description: 'The council gatherings where Dakota leaders made decisions of war, peace, and alliance'

memes.label: Unktéhee
memes.description: 'The Great Water Spirit of Dakota mythology — creator of the earth, dwelling beneath the Falls of St. Anthony, in perpetual opposition to the Thunder-Bird Wakínyan'

memes.label: Virgin Star
memes.description: "The Dakota name for the Evening Star, believed to be the spirit of a virgin wronged at the ceremonial feast, identified with the maiden Wiwástè in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Virgins' Dance"
memes.description: 'The ceremonial dance of Dakota maidens at the Feast of the Virgins'

memes.label: Voyageurs
memes.description: "French fur traders and explorers of the upper Mississippi — the first Europeans to contact the Dakotas, who gave them the name Sioux"

memes.label: Wa-zí-ya
memes.description: 'The Dakota god of Winter and the North Wind, also called the "Winter-maker." His star is the North Star, and his home is at I-tó-ka-ga Wi-câs-ta in the frozen North. The Ojibways call him Ka-be-bón-ik-ka.'

memes.label: Wakan
memes.description: 'The Dakota concept of sacred or mysterious power, manifested in spirits, medicine, and ritual'

memes.label: Wakan Teepee
memes.description: "The sacred lodge or ceremonial space of the Dakotas — site of the Wakan Wacipee (Sacred Dance) and burial place of chiefs; also the Dakota name for Carver's Cave at St. Paul"

memes.label: "Wakan Teepee (Site)"
memes.description: 'The Sacred Lodge site where Dakota kindred gathered (015-242), a place of spiritual significance'

memes.label: Wakâwa
memes.description: 'A tall Dakota chief who presides over the courtship rituals in "The Feast of the Virgins" — father of a daughter courted by rival suitors, arbiter of the legend''s central conflicts.'

memes.label: Wanagee-Tach-anku
memes.description: 'The Pathway of the Spirits — the Dakota name for the Milky Way, believed to be the path over which spirits of the dead travel to the Spirit-land'

memes.label: Wanâta
memes.description: 'A tall young Dakota warrior chosen as Itâncan (chief) of his band — central figure in the opening of "The Legend of the Falls," whose fate is entangled with Apè-dúta.'

memes.label: "Wapasa"
memes.description: "Dakota chief and grandfather figure mentioned in Gordon's legends"

memes.label: "Warrior's Death Ritual"
memes.description: 'The ritual preparations and songs of Dakota warriors before death in battle'

memes.label: Waub-Ojeeg
memes.description: 'A famous Ojibway warrior and chief, father of the Sea-Gull — renowned for his martial prowess and pride in "The Legend of the Falls."'

memes.label: Wazí-kuté
memes.description: A tall Dakota chief, father of Tamdóka — his name and lineage are invoked

memes.label: Wigwam
memes.description: 'The traditional Ojibway (Chippewa) dome-shaped dwelling, appearing in the Sea-Gull and Red Fox legends as the home of key characters'

memes.label: "Winona"
memes.description: "The central heroine of the longest legend cycle in Gordon's collection — a Dakota maiden of legendary beauty whose tragic leap rather than marry outside her people is the climactic event of the legends"

memes.label: Winona's Leap
memes.description: The tragic death of Winona, the Dakota maiden who leaped to her death

memes.label: "Winter Camp"
memes.description: 'The seasonal winter encampment of Dakota bands, a recurring event in the annual cycle'

memes.label: Wiwásté
memes.description: 'The fairest Dakota maiden, daughter of a Dakota chief — central figure in "The Feast of the Virgins" legend, courted by many warriors.'
